## Overview

This repository contains **Terraform DigitalOcean Components**, a collection of centralized infrastructure components built by combining multiple DigitalOcean modules in one place.

It is intended to simplify deployment and management of shared infrastructure by calling the required modules together through a single component-based implementation.

By default, this repository provisions multiple DigitalOcean components available under the `terraform/modules/` directory.

---

## Available Components

| COMPONENT | PATH | URL |
|:----------|:-----|:------------|
| VPC | [terraform/modules/vpc](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-components/tree/master/terraform/modules/vpc) | [terraform-digitalocean-vpc](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-vpc) |
| DROPLET | [terraform/modules/droplet](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-components/tree/master/terraform/modules/droplet) | [terraform-digitalocean-droplet](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-droplet) |
| CDN | [terraform/modules/cdn](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-components/tree/master/terraform/modules/cdn) | [terraform-digitalocean-cdn](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-cdn) |
| CERTIFICATE | [terraform/modules/certificate](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-components/tree/master/terraform/modules/certificate) | [terraform-digitalocean-certificate](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-certificate) |
| DATABASE | [terraform/modules/database](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-components/tree/master/terraform/modules/database) | [terraform-digitalocean-database](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-database) |
| DOMAIN | [terraform/modules/domain](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-components/tree/master/terraform/modules/domain) | [terraform-digitalocean-domain](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-domain) |
| SPACES | [terraform/modules/spaces](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-components/tree/master/terraform/modules/spaces) | [terraform-digitalocean-spaces](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-spaces) |
| KUBERNETES | [terraform/modules/kubernetes](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-components/tree/master/terraform/modules/kubernetes) | [terraform-digitalocean-kubernetes](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-kubernetes) |
| CONTAINER-REGISTRY | [terraform/modules/container-registry](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-components/tree/master/terraform/modules/container-registry) | [terraform-digitalocean-container-registry](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-container-registry) |
| FIREWALL | [terraform/modules/firewall](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-components/tree/master/terraform/modules/firewall) | [terraform-digitalocean-firewall](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-firewall) |
| MONITORING | [terraform/modules/monitoring](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-components/tree/master/terraform/modules/monitoring) | [terraform-digitalocean-monitoring](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-monitoring) |
| LOADBALANCER | [terraform/modules/loadbalancer](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-components/tree/master/terraform/modules/loadbalancer) | [terraform-digitalocean-loadbalancer](https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-loadbalancer) |



## Prerequisites and Providers

This table contains both prerequisites and supported tooling:

| Description   | Name      | Version   |
|---------------|-----------|-----------|
| Prerequisite  | Terraform | >= 1.6.0  |
| Prerequisite  | OpenTofu  | >= 1.6.0  |

---

## What This Repository Deploys

By default, this component repository can provision multiple DigitalOcean components listed in the `module/` directory.

This approach helps you:

- Deploy centralized resources consistently
- Reduce duplication across environments
- Reuse multiple modules in a single setup
- Standardize infrastructure provisioning patterns

---

## Quick Start Guide

Navigate to your environment directory:
```
cd terraform/sandbox/blr1
```

### Terraform Commands

-   **Initialize:** Sets up the working directory and downloads providers.

    ```
    terraform init
    ```

-   **Plan:** Previews the changes Terraform will make to your infrastructure.

    ```
    terraform plan
    ```

-   **Apply:** Executes the changes to provision or update resources.

    ```
    terraform apply
    ```

-   **Destroy:** Terminates and cleans up all managed resources.

    ```
    terraform destroy
    ```

### Enable / Disable Modules

To prevent a module from being provisioned, simply set the `enabled` variable to `false` in the module block:

```
module "example" {
  source  = "..."
  enabled = false
}
```

Explore implementation patterns and deployment examples in the [`examples/`](./examples/) directory.

These examples demonstrate how to:

- Call multiple modules together
- Deploy shared infrastructure resources
- Follow standardized component-based patterns

## ✨ Contributors

Big thanks to our contributors for elevating this project with their dedication and expertise.

We welcome community contributions to improve these components and make them more versatile and useful for everyone.

<div align="center">
  <a href="https://github.com/terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-components/graphs/contributors" title="Contributors">
    <img src="https://contrib.rocks/image?repo=terraform-do-modules/terraform-digitalocean-components" />
  </a>
</div>

If you're considering contributing, please follow these guidelines:

- **Fork the Repository:** Create a new branch for your feature or bug fix
- **Coding Standards:** Follow the existing code style and project conventions
- **Clear Commit Messages:** Write clear and concise commit messages
- **Thorough Testing:** Test your changes before submitting a pull request
- **Documentation Updates:** Update documentation when changes affect usage

**Note:** All contributors must follow the [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org) specification for commit messages to ensure consistency and better versioning.

## Notes

- Do not use the `master` (or `main`) branch for production deployments
- Always reference a stable version using Git tags or official releases
- Using tagged versions ensures consistency, stability, and reproducible deployments
- Avoid consuming unversioned or in-progress code
